mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nominal retail sales forecasts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because consumer spending directly drives top-line corporate revenue and earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS prints coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: A civilian unemployment rate above 9.5 percent signals excess labor slack that suppresses wage income and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Months with UNRATE above 9.5 percent are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production over three months and increases in unemployment capture a broad macroeconomic contraction that triggers risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change negative and UNRATE 3-month change positive
failure_condition: When both INDPRO is falling over three months and UNRATE is rising over three months, the S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 45 percent of joint-signal observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound regime where the federal funds rate is below 0.25 percent, month-over-month industrial production growth signals successful monetary policy transmission and reflation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; this mechanism is inactive outside that regime.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP of more than 1.5 percent annualized forecasts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because aggregate output growth directly maps to corporate earnings capacity and margin expansion.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth above 1.5 percent annualized are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the opportunity cost of equity capital and compresses short-term discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Months with a decline in FEDFUNDS coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration of housing starts above 550 thousand units signals a bottoming in residential construction and positive wealth effects that lift cyclical equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Months with HOUST above 550 thousand are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized consumer price inflation above 4 percent over a three-month window compresses real corporate margins and raises the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation above 4 percent coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered observations.
